**To restore the React application to the live domain:**

1. **Verify Netlify deployment:** Confirm actual Netlify deployment URL and that the application is correctly built and deployed
2. **Update DNS configuration:** Point `kungalvskott.se` to Netlify (update CNAME to Netlify deployment domain)
3. **Verify SPA routing:** Confirm SPA fallback and asset serving work correctly once DNS routes to Netlify
4. **Decommission legacy hosting:** Once live domain routes to Netlify, legacy PHP/Loopia hos

### Netlify
- Static site hosting with CDN
- Automated deployments from git push
- Serverless Functions for backend logic (form processing, etc.)
- Edge Functions for request-level security logic
- Preview deployments for branch/PR testing
- Production domain supports custom domain CNAME configuration

### Legacy Loopia Hosting
- Shared PHP hosting infrastructure
- Still serving the old application code
- No connection to React/Vite build system
- Blocks modern deployment on the user-reported do

### Netlify Deployment (`netlify.toml`)

**Build Configuration:**
```toml
[build]
command = "npm run build"
publish = "dist"
```

**Production Environment (PRERENDER_ENABLED=true):**
- Prerendering enabled for critical routes
- Reduces Time to First Contentful Paint (FCP)
- Generates static HTML for Server-Side Rendering (SSR)-like experience on select routes

**SPA Fallback Redirect:**
```toml
[[redirects]]
from = "/*"
to = "/index.html"
status = 200
```
Ensures all non-matching routes return `

Investigation determined that the reported broken assets on https://kungalvskott.se are not due to missing or misconfigured static files, but rather a **DNS routing configuration mismatch**. The live domain currently resolves to legacy PHP/Loopia hosting infrastructure instead of the modern Vite-built React application deployed on Netlify.

Complete SEO infrastructure + content deployed across all 10 Webraketen sites in one session.

### What was done:
- Audited all 10 repos for SEO infrastructure gaps

- Added cookie consent to kraftrent + tittisstad
- Added Sharp image optimization to 6 Astro sites

**Webraketen client site** — managed under the Webraketen agency.

- **Project:** webraketen-monitor (ID: 860840487775)
- **Service account:** webraketen@webraketen-monitor.iam.gserviceaccount.com
- **Service account key:** ~/.config/gcloud/webraketen-service-account.json
- **ADC credentials:** ~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json

### Build Tool & Frontend Framework
- **Framework:** React 18.3.1 with React DOM 18.3.1
- **Build Tool:** Vite with React plugin for client-side SPA bundling
- **Build Output:** Static assets in `/dist/` directory
- **Prerendering:** Enabled via `scripts/prerender-critical-routes.mjs` for critical routes

### Build Pipeline
```
npm run build
├── vite build (bundles React application to dist/)
└── node scripts/prerender-critical-routes.mjs (generates static HTML for critical routes)
```

name: feedback_raket_crawler_no_ai
description: Always crawl with --no-ai flag using raket-crawler, do extraction in-session instead
type: feedback
---

Always use `--no-ai` when running raket-crawler. Do AI extraction in the Claude Code session instead of via the crawler's built-in API call.

**Why:** Avoids burning separate Anthropic API credits when Claude Code can do the same extraction in-session for free. The `--no-ai` flag should be the only option — don't even present AI extraction as an

name: feedback_self_improvement
description: Self-improvement has two distinct modes — surface maintenance vs deep behavioral evolution. Never conflate them.
type: feedback
---

"Self improve" means two completely different things. Don't mix them.

**Surface maintenance** — config hygiene, temp file cleanup, stale session purge, uptime checks. This is janitorial. Useful but not growth. Run it on autopilot.

**Real self-improvement** — analyze past mistakes, study correction patterns, review git

name: feedback_always_commit_push
description: Always commit and push after making file changes — don't wait to be asked
type: feedback
---

After making any file changes, always commit and push without waiting to be asked.

**Why:** User explicitly stated "ofc, always" when asked if they wanted changes pushed.

**How to apply:** As soon as edits are done, commit and push. For local files not yet in a repo, find the correct GitHub repo (check memory/ask) and push via GitHub MCP push_files.

name: feedback_check_before_asking

---

Before asking ANY clarifying question, exhaust available context first:
1. Invoke relevant skills (Skill tool)
2. Check MEMORY.md for prior context
3. Scan available MCPs for matching tools
4. Check `~/.secrets` for credentials

Only ask if something is genuinely missing after all of the above.

**Why:** Christoffer asked Claude to do something

**How to apply:** Any time you

name: webraketen_csp
description: Webraketen CSP setup — which pages need their own override and why
type: project
---

The global `/*` CSP in `netlify.toml` uses sha256 hashes for all scripts. Any page whose Astro-bundled JS changes (i.e. after editing the `.astro` file) will break silently — the browser blocks the script, JS never runs.

**Why:** Astro produces a new bundle hash on every rebuild. That hash isn't in the global hash list → script blocked → no JS = forms submit natively (page rel

name: Ads MCPs (Meta + Google via Pipeboard)
description: Pipeboard MCP endpoints for Meta Ads and Google Ads — both use PIPEBOARD_TOKEN from ~/.secrets
type: reference
---

Both ad platform MCPs run via Pipeboard remote HTTP — no local server needed.

- **Meta Ads MCP**: `https://mcp.pipeboard.co/meta-ads-mcp?token=${PIPEBOARD_TOKEN}`
- **Google Ads MCP**: `https://google-ads.mcp.pipeboard.co/?token=${PIPEBOARD_TOKEN}`

Token stored in `~/.secrets` as `PIPEBOARD_TOKEN`.
